# Hearing Manifest
<!-- Machine-readable index. One row per Judicial Hearing. -->
<!-- Written by /judicial hear when a ruling is recorded. Do not edit manually. -->
<!-- corpus.py and bash_write_guard.py read this file for enforcement. -->
<!--                                                                              -->
<!-- Column definitions:                                                          -->
<!--   ID       — H-NNN, sequential                                               -->
<!--   Name     — short hearing name (no commas)                                  -->
<!--   Date     — YYYY-MM-DD                                                      -->
<!--   Files    — comma-separated list of Layer 2 files covered                   -->
<!--   Has-A    — TRUE if Attorney-A argued section is present and non-empty      -->
<!--   Has-B    — TRUE if Attorney-B argued section is present and non-empty      -->
<!--   Has-J    — TRUE if Justice ruled section is present and non-empty          -->
<!--   Status   — OPEN (ruling recorded) | CLOSED (frozen by stage-compactor)    -->

| ID | Name | Date | Files | Has-A | Has-B | Has-J | Status |
|----|------|------|-------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
